title: podman build cannot read from /sys/fs/selinux/
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot run `useradd` inside a CentOS 6 container due to permission problems with /sys/fs/selinux/ 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
(executed on a standard Fedora 31 with SElinux enabled, podman version 2.04)

1. Add the following Dockerfile (strace only for demonstrating the issue)
```
FROM centos:6
RUN ls -lZ /sys/fs/
RUN yum install strace -y
RUN strace -eopen useradd foo
```

2. Build the dockerfile: `podman build  --cap-add SYS_PTRACE .`

**Describe the results you received:**

The standard FS permissions look ok for the folder:
```
drwx-----T. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:bpf_t:s0       bpf
dr-xr-xr-x. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:cgroup_t:s0    cgroup
drwxr-xr-x. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:sysfs_t:s0     ext4
drwxr-xr-x. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:sysfs_t:s0     fuse
drwxr-x---. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:pstore_t:s0    pstore
drwxr-xr-x. 65534 65534 system_u:object_r:security_t:s0  selinux
```

But `useradd` fails when it tries to open a file inside `/sys/fs/selinux`

```
open("/sys/fs/selinux/enforce", O_RDONLY) = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)
useradd: cannot set SELinux context for home directory /home/foo
+++ exited with 12 +++
Error: error building at STEP "RUN strace -eopen useradd foo": error while running runtime: exit status 12
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect the whole thing to work just fine.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I can run the command `useradd` fine when I invoke `podman run centos:6`, so I suspect this has something to do with how `podman build` mounts the filesystem.

Interestingly, the whole thing works just fine with a CentOS 7 container. Maybe the `useradd` behavior becaome a little bit more resilient, but still I wonder why the fs is mounted into the build

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman-2.0.4-1.fc31.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Could not test latest version. Troubleshooting guide did not help.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** 

This is a physical system.

comments.body: This is fixed once buildah is updated, waiting for buildah 1.16.1 to be cut by @TomSweeneyRedHat
I believe Buildah 1.16.1 has been vendored into Podman, going to close.
